name: ollama-codex
description: An in-Codex visual control panel and command layer for configuring Codex App and Codex CLI with Ollama.

interface.shortDescription: Use a Codex Mac app GUI to manage Ollama options for Codex.
interface.longDescription: Ollama can already work with Codex, but discovery is low and the command surface is tedious to remember. Ollama for Codex gives Ollama a visual, reversible home inside the Codex Mac app: an MCP-powered model switcher and control panel, starter prompts, slash commands, status checks, dry runs, model helpers, and explicit restore flows. Open `/ollama-codex-panel` to render the panel in chat, inspect Ollama readiness, choose from local models or Ollama Cloud presets with direct Use controls, pull a model, configure Codex App, configure Codex CLI, and restore either profile. For Codex App, the backend uses `ollama launch codex-app --yes`, switches to a local or Ollama Cloud model with `ollama launch codex-app --model <model> --yes`, and restores the previous App profile with `ollama launch codex-app --restore --yes`. For Codex CLI, it can create a non-launching `ollama-launch` profile for a chosen model, restore via `ollama launch codex --restore`, prepare terminal commands for `codex --oss`, use a specific local or cloud model with `codex --oss -m <model>`, or use the generated `ollama-launch` profile with `codex --profile ollama-launch`. The plugin preserves App/CLI profile separation, delegates Codex App configuration to Ollama, and requires explicit confirmation before actions that may restart Codex or restore profile state.
defaultPrompt: Switch Codex App to an Ollama model
